name: drift-detection scan supports a post-commit mode that limits the git scan to the most recent commit by default
command: mkdir -p repo && (cd repo && git init -q && touch a && git -c user.email=x@y -c user.name=x add a && git -c user.email=x@y -c user.name=x commit -q -m 'first' && touch b && git -c user.email=x@y -c user.name=x add b && git -c user.email=x@y -c user.name=x commit -q --no-verify -m 'second skipped') && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/skills/drift-detection/scan.sh" --git-root repo --post-commit --out report.jsonl 2>err.txt; rc=$?; [ "$rc" -ne 0 ] && grep -q 'no_verify' report.jsonl && grep -q '"commits_scanned":1' err.txt && echo flagged 1>&2
